unverified_claims.note: Calculated as the percentage change from an estimated $4B digital human market in 2021 to $47.72B in 2025 (Mordor Intelligence). The 2021 baseline is not directly sourced — it is derived by back-calculating the 40.3% CAGR from 2025 figures. No single source provides a verified 2021 digital human market size. The +1,093% figure is directionally accurate but the exact 2021 baseline is estimated. Some research firms use narrower 'AI avatar' definitions that would produce a smaller but potentially higher % growth.

This figure uses HeyGen's ARR growth from $57.5M to $95M between January and September 2025 (~9 months, +65%) as a sector momentum proxy. It is NOT a quarterly sequential market share figure — no published source provides Q-over-Q AI avatar market growth for a 3-month window. The ARR growth rate of leading vendors is used as the best available leading indicator of short-term market momentum.

No primary keyword tool (Keywords Everywhere, Ahrefs, SEMrush) was directly queried. The ~200K estimate combines 'AI video generation' (high volume), 'AI avatars' (growing), 'digital humans AI', and 'HeyGen' brand searches. The actual combined volume may be higher if 'Synthesia' brand searches and 'AI presenter video' variants are included. A direct keyword tool query is needed for a verified figure.

unverified_claims.claim: Market share percentages for players (Synthesia ~25%, HeyGen ~18%, etc.)
unverified_claims.note: No single analyst has published explicit market share percentages for the AI digital human sub-segment. Shares are estimated by triangulating ARR figures (Synthesia $146M, HeyGen $100M), customer counts, and total market revenue from MarketsandMarkets and Mordor Intelligence. These are directional estimates for relative competitive positioning only.

unverified_claims.claim: Klarna produced 1,000 product videos in 37 languages in days
unverified_claims.note: Referenced in multiple secondary sources as a HeyGen customer case study but not directly verified against a primary Klarna or HeyGen press release. Listed as [unverified] — the directional claim (enterprise-scale multilingual video production via AI avatars) is well corroborated; the specific numbers may vary.
